introSection: Impulse is a steel roller coaster located at Knoebels Amusement Resort in Elysburg, Pennsylvania, USA.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The first pieces of track for Impulse arrived in January 2015.

The ride opened on April 25, 2015.

sections.title: Layout
sections.content: After dispatching from the station, the trains go through a 90 degree, right-hand turn, that leads into a straight section of track facing riders toward the vertical lift hill. After completing the lift hill, the trains plunge down a 90 degree drop which leads into a cobra roll. After exiting the cobra roll, riders will experience a vertical loop, similar to the vertical loops seen on early inversions of the early 1900's, and not like an eliptical loop on modern B&M or Intamin roller coasters. After these inversions, riders will experience an overbanked turn into an in-line twist, which leads into a helix, and finally the brake run.

fullText:
Impulse is a steel roller coaster located at Knoebels Amusement Resort in Elysburg, Pennsylvania, USA.

The first pieces of track for Impulse arrived in January 2015.

The ride opened on April 25, 2015.



After dispatching from the station, the trains go through a 90 degree, right-hand turn, that leads into a straight section of track facing riders toward the vertical lift hill. After completing the lift hill, the trains plunge down a 90 degree drop which leads into a cobra roll. After exiting the cobra roll, riders will experience a vertical loop, similar to the vertical loops seen on early inversions of the early 1900's, and not like an eliptical loop on modern B&M or Intamin roller coasters. After these inversions, riders will experience an overbanked turn into an in-line twist, which leads into a helix, and finally the brake run.
